close_reason: Added with_connected_state() wrapper that clears client on transport errors
description: ensure_connected (lib.rs:268) only connects when state.client is None. nvm_list/nvm_metadata/nvm_read/nvm_overwrite/nvm_add can fail with Error::Ffi (transport-level: e.g. underlying socket closed, server killed and restarted). Those errors propagate via backend_err but state.client stays Some, so subsequent calls reuse the dead client and fail identically forever. Trigger: HSM server restarts during the lifetime of a long-running secretx process -> all subsequent get/put calls error permanently with no path to recovery short of dropping the WolfHsmBackend and reconstructing it. Fix: on failed RPC where the wolfhsm::Error indicates likely transport breakage (Error::Ffi, or specific WH codes like NOTREADY/ABORTED/TIMEOUT), set state.client = None so the next call reconnects.
title: Broken connection never reset; transport-level errors leave state.client = Some forever
